report_metadata.stage: T4aN3bM0 (AJCC 8th Edition Stage III for p16+ OPSCC)

treatments.name: Definitive Cisplatin-based Concurrent Chemoradiation (70 Gy + Cisplatin 100mg/m2 q3w)

evidence_level.rationale: Multiple Phase 3 RCTs (RTOG 1016, De-ESCALaTE) confirm cisplatin-CRT as gold standard for HPV+ OPSCC; NCCN Category 1 recommendation; strongest level of evidence

survival_benefit.rationale: 5-year OS 84.6% with cisplatin-CRT in HPV+ OPSCC (RTOG 1016); for unfavorable T4aN3b disease, 3-year OS approximately 70-75%; de-escalation NOT appropriate for this high-volume disease

accessibility.rationale: Widely available at all radiation oncology centers; cisplatin is a generic, inexpensive agent; standard treatment infrastructure

safety_profile.rationale: Significant acute toxicity including grade 3+ mucositis (40-50%), nephrotoxicity, ototoxicity, myelosuppression; cumulative dose of 200mg/m2 target; requires aggressive supportive care

biomarker_match.rationale: Standard for all HPV+ OPSCC; SMAD4 loss does not alter indication for cisplatin-CRT; PIK3CA mutation does not change primary treatment recommendation

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Cisplatin forms DNA adducts and cross-links that inhibit DNA replication and repair, acting as a potent radiosensitizer. Combined with 70 Gy intensity-modulated radiation therapy (IMRT) in 35 fractions, this provides definitive locoregional tumor control through synergistic cytotoxicity.

treatments.name: Neoadjuvant + Adjuvant Pembrolizumab with Standard CRT (KEYNOTE-689 Regimen)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 KEYNOTE-689 trial published NEJM 2025; FDA-approved June 2025 for PD-L1 CPS>=1 resectable LA-HNSCC; HR 0.66 for event-free survival

survival_benefit.rationale: 36-month EFS 57.6% vs 46.4% (total population); HR 0.66 for CPS>=10; median DFS 51.8 vs 35.7 months; significant improvement over standard alone

accessibility.rationale: FDA-approved as of June 2025; requires PD-L1 testing; available at oncology centers; high cost of immunotherapy but growing insurance coverage

safety_profile.rationale: Grade 3+ TRAEs 44.6% vs 42.9% with SOC alone; immune-related AEs (pneumonitis, hepatitis, colitis); treatment-related death 1.1%; manageable with monitoring

biomarker_match.rationale: Requires PD-L1 CPS>=1; HPV+ tumors often express PD-L1; SMAD4 loss and PIK3CA mutation may influence checkpoint response but data are limited

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Pembrolizumab is an anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody that blocks the PD-1/PD-L1 immune checkpoint, restoring T-cell-mediated anti-tumor immunity. Neoadjuvant pembrolizumab (2 cycles before surgery/CRT) primes immune responses, while adjuvant pembrolizumab (15 cycles) maintains immune surveillance against micrometastatic disease.

treatments.name: Induction Chemotherapy (TPF) Followed by Concurrent Chemoradiation

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 data (TAX-323, TAX-324) showing TPF superior to PF induction; used for high-volume disease to reduce tumor burden before definitive CRT; not universally standard but NCCN Category 2B

survival_benefit.rationale: May improve outcomes in bulky T4aN3b disease by reducing tumor burden before CRT; 5-year OS 27.5% vs 18.6% for TPF vs PF in advanced HNSCC overall; HPV+ cohort does better

accessibility.rationale: Available at oncology centers; requires 3 cycles of TPF with aggressive supportive care before starting CRT; extends total treatment duration

safety_profile.rationale: Significant toxicity: febrile neutropenia, mucositis, diarrhea with TPF; may compromise ability to complete subsequent CRT; requires G-CSF support and meticulous care

biomarker_match.rationale: Appropriate for high-volume T4aN3b disease regardless of molecular profile; may help debulk disease before radiation

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Sequential approach: 3 cycles of docetaxel, cisplatin, and 5-fluorouracil (TPF) provide rapid tumor cytoreduction, followed by definitive concurrent chemoradiation. Induction chemotherapy reduces tumor burden, potentially improving locoregional control and reducing distant failure in high-volume disease.

key_evidence.journal: New England Journal of Medicine / Journal of Clinical Oncology

treatments.name: Pembrolizumab + Chemotherapy (for Recurrent/Metastatic Setting - KEYNOTE-048)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 KEYNOTE-048 trial; FDA-approved first-line for R/M HNSCC; definitive evidence for pembrolizumab with or without chemotherapy based on CPS score

survival_benefit.rationale: Median OS 13.0 months with pembrolizumab + chemo vs 10.7 months with EXTREME; ORR 36% in combination; improved outcomes in PD-L1+ HPV+ subgroup

accessibility.rationale: FDA-approved first-line for R/M HNSCC; widely available at oncology centers; PD-L1 testing required for monotherapy selection

safety_profile.rationale: Lower toxicity than EXTREME regimen; immune-related AEs manageable; 17% ORR with monotherapy in CPS>=20 with better safety profile

biomarker_match.rationale: HPV+ tumors have higher response rates to pembrolizumab (25-32% vs 14% HPV-); PD-L1 expression guides monotherapy vs combination selection

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Pembrolizumab monotherapy or combined with platinum/5-FU chemotherapy blocks PD-1/PD-L1 checkpoint, restoring anti-tumor immunity. In HPV+ disease, viral antigens provide targets for immune recognition. Chemotherapy may enhance immunogenicity through immunogenic cell death.
notable_side_effects: When combined with chemo: myelosuppression, nephrotoxicity

treatments.name: Alpelisib (PI3Kα Inhibitor) for PIK3CA-Mutant Disease

evidence_level.rationale: TRIUMPH Phase 2 trial in R/M HNSCC with PI3K pathway alterations; differential efficacy by mutation site; FDA-approved for PIK3CA-mutant breast cancer; off-label for HNSCC

survival_benefit.rationale: PFS 7.3 months for helical domain mutations (E542/E545) vs 1.6 months for H1047 kinase domain mutations; mutation site matters significantly; overall modest benefit

accessibility.rationale: FDA-approved for PIK3CA-mutant breast cancer; oral medication; off-label use for HNSCC possible; requires PIK3CA mutation testing

safety_profile.rationale: Hyperglycemia (most common, dose-limiting), rash, diarrhea, nausea; manageable with dose adjustments and concomitant metformin; requires glucose monitoring

biomarker_match.rationale: Directly targets PIK3CA mutation present in this patient; specific mutation site (helical vs kinase domain) determines efficacy; highly biomarker-matched therapy

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Alpelisib selectively inhibits PI3Kα (p110α), blocking the PI3K/AKT/mTOR signaling pathway activated by PIK3CA mutations. This suppresses cell proliferation, survival signaling, and may overcome resistance to other targeted therapies in PIK3CA-mutant tumors.
biomarker_requirements: Mutation site determination (helical vs kinase domain)
treatments.availability: FDA Approved (off-label for HNSCC; approved for breast cancer)

treatments.name: Nivolumab (CheckMate-141 Regimen for R/M Disease)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 CheckMate-141 trial; FDA-approved for platinum-refractory R/M HNSCC; HR 0.60 for OS in HPV+ subgroup

survival_benefit.rationale: Median OS 7.5 months vs 5.1 months with standard chemo; 40% reduction in death risk; 1-year OS 36% vs 17%; HPV+ patients showed greatest benefit (HR 0.60)

accessibility.rationale: FDA-approved for platinum-refractory R/M HNSCC; widely available; established dosing and monitoring protocols

safety_profile.rationale: Grade 3-4 TRAEs in 13% vs 35% with standard chemotherapy; significantly better toxicity profile; immune-related AEs manageable

biomarker_match.rationale: HPV+ tumors show greater OS benefit (HR 0.60); PD-L1 expressors derive additional benefit; relevant for this HPV+ patient

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Nivolumab is an anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody that blocks PD-1/PD-L1 interaction, reinvigorating exhausted T cells to mount anti-tumor immune responses. Particularly effective in HPV+ tumors where viral antigens serve as immune targets.

treatments.name: PDS0101 (HPV16 Therapeutic Vaccine) + Pembrolizumab

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 window-of-opportunity trial with early results; durable clinical responses in HPV+ R/M HNSCC; promising but not yet Phase 3 validated

survival_benefit.rationale: Durable clinical responses observed in HPV+ R/M HNSCC; enhanced T-cell responses to HPV antigens; potential to improve outcomes when combined with checkpoint inhibition

accessibility.rationale: Clinical trial access only; Phase 2 studies at select academic centers; not yet commercially available

safety_profile.rationale: Therapeutic vaccine with generally favorable safety profile; injection site reactions; immune activation well-tolerated; enhanced by combination with pembrolizumab

biomarker_match.rationale: Specifically targets HPV16 E6/E7 oncoproteins; directly relevant to this HPV+ patient; T-cell stimulating immunotherapy targeting the viral driver

treatments.mechanism_of_action: PDS0101 is a therapeutic vaccine that stimulates T-cell responses against HPV16 E6 and E7 oncoproteins using a novel lipid-based delivery platform. Combined with anti-PD-1 checkpoint inhibition, it aims to generate robust, durable anti-tumor immunity specific to HPV-driven cancers.

key_evidence.study_name: MC200710 Phase 2 Window of Opportunity Trial

treatments.name: Carboplatin-based Concurrent Chemoradiation (Cisplatin-Ineligible Alternative)

evidence_level.rationale: Multiple retrospective and prospective studies; NCCN-recommended alternative for cisplatin-ineligible patients; inferior to cisplatin-CRT in head-to-head comparisons

survival_benefit.rationale: Inferior to cisplatin-CRT (HR approximately 1.25-1.30 for OS); 5-year OS approximately 75% vs 85% with cisplatin; acceptable alternative when cisplatin contraindicated

accessibility.rationale: Widely available; better tolerated renally than cisplatin; can be given with less aggressive hydration; suitable for patients with renal impairment or hearing loss

safety_profile.rationale: Less nephrotoxicity and ototoxicity than cisplatin; more myelosuppression; generally better tolerated; enables treatment completion in cisplatin-ineligible patients

biomarker_match.rationale: Non-specific radiosensitizer; appropriate for any HNSCC regardless of molecular profile; second-line platinum option

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Carboplatin forms DNA cross-links and adducts similar to cisplatin but with a different toxicity profile. Used as a radiosensitizer at AUC 1.5-2 weekly or AUC 5 every 3 weeks concurrent with definitive IMRT (70 Gy), providing locoregional control with more manageable renal and ototoxicity.

key_evidence.study_name: Survival After Definitive CRT With Concurrent Cisplatin or Carboplatin for HNSCC
key_evidence.journal: Journal of the National Comprehensive Cancer Network

treatments.name: Transoral Robotic Surgery (TORS) + Adjuvant Therapy

evidence_level.rationale: Multiple Phase 2 trials and retrospective data; ASCO guideline published 2024; established for T1-T2 OPSCC; T4a is generally a contraindication for primary surgery

survival_benefit.rationale: 5-year DSS 88.7% in high-risk groups; however, T4a disease is generally NOT surgical and represents a contraindication; risk of positive margins and need for adjuvant CRT negates de-escalation benefit

accessibility.rationale: Requires da Vinci robotic system and trained surgeon; available at major academic centers; T4a generally not offered surgical approach

safety_profile.rationale: Surgical complications include hemorrhage, dysphagia, fistula; for T4a, risk of significant morbidity from extensive resection; likely requires adjuvant CRT regardless, adding combined toxicity

biomarker_match.rationale: HPV+ status favors surgical approach in appropriate candidates; however, T4a N3b represents advanced disease where primary surgery is NOT standard; better suited for T1-T2 disease

treatments.mechanism_of_action: TORS provides minimally invasive transoral access to oropharyngeal tumors using robotic-assisted surgery with high-definition 3D visualization. For appropriate candidates, primary surgical resection may allow de-escalation of adjuvant therapy, though T4a disease generally requires full-dose adjuvant CRT regardless.

key_evidence.study_name: ASCO Guideline on TORS in OPSCC; Multiple institutional series

biomarker_requirements: Surgeon assessment of resectability — T4a generally contraindicated
notable_side_effects: Need for adjuvant CRT in high-risk features

treatments.name: Cetuximab + Radiation (NOT Recommended — Inferior to Cisplatin)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 RTOG 1016 and De-ESCALaTE conclusively demonstrated cetuximab-RT INFERIOR to cisplatin-CRT in HPV+ OPSCC; high-quality negative evidence

survival_benefit.rationale: 5-year OS 77.9% vs 84.6% with cisplatin; significantly worse PFS (67.3% vs 78.4%); higher locoregional failure; De-ESCALaTE 2-year OS 89.4% vs 97.5%; NOT recommended

accessibility.rationale: Cetuximab widely available; only appropriate for cisplatin-ineligible patients; SMAD4 loss further argues against cetuximab due to predicted resistance

safety_profile.rationale: Less nephro/ototoxicity than cisplatin; acneiform rash, infusion reactions; similar overall moderate-severe toxicity rates; better tolerated but inferior efficacy

biomarker_match.rationale: SMAD4 loss specifically associated with cetuximab resistance and MAPK/JNK activation; PIK3CA mutation may further reduce cetuximab efficacy; CONTRAINDICATED in this molecular profile

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Cetuximab is an anti-EGFR monoclonal antibody that blocks EGFR signaling and activates antibody-dependent cellular cytotoxicity (ADCC). However, RTOG 1016 and De-ESCALaTE demonstrated it is INFERIOR to cisplatin as a radiosensitizer in HPV+ OPSCC, and SMAD4 loss specifically predicts cetuximab resistance.

key_evidence.study_name: RTOG 1016 and De-ESCALaTE HPV Phase 3 Trials

biomarker_requirements: SMAD4 status should be checked — loss predicts resistance
treatments.availability: FDA Approved (but NOT recommended over cisplatin in HPV+ OPSCC)

treatments.name: ISA101b (HPV Therapeutic Vaccine) + Cemiplimab

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 study ongoing; HPV therapeutic vaccine approach combined with anti-PD-1; early data showing immune responses; not yet Phase 3 validated

survival_benefit.rationale: Early clinical data showing objective responses in HPV+ R/M HNSCC; potential for durable responses through combined viral antigen targeting and checkpoint blockade

accessibility.rationale: Clinical trial access only; limited centers; not commercially available

safety_profile.rationale: Therapeutic vaccine generally well-tolerated; injection site reactions; immune-related AEs from cemiplimab manageable

biomarker_match.rationale: Specifically targets HPV E6/E7 viral antigens; directly relevant to HPV+ disease; combined with checkpoint inhibition for synergistic anti-tumor immunity

treatments.mechanism_of_action: ISA101b is a synthetic long peptide vaccine targeting HPV16 E6 and E7 oncoproteins. Combined with cemiplimab (anti-PD-1), it generates HPV-specific T-cell responses while removing immune checkpoints, creating a targeted immunotherapy approach for HPV-driven cancers.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase 2 Study of ISA101b + Cemiplimab in HPV+ OPSCC

treatments.name: EGFR + JNK/MAPK Dual Inhibition (for SMAD4-loss Tumors)

evidence_level.rationale: Preclinical data from Moffitt Cancer Center/Johns Hopkins; SMAD4 loss activates MAPK/JNK bypass pathway; dual inhibition proposed but not yet in clinical trials for HNSCC

survival_benefit.rationale: Preclinical rationale strong: SMAD4 loss causes cetuximab resistance via MAPK/JNK activation; concomitant inhibition restores sensitivity in vitro; no clinical data yet

accessibility.rationale: Research only; individual MAPK and JNK inhibitors exist but combination not studied clinically in HNSCC; would require clinical trial access

safety_profile.rationale: MEK inhibitors cause skin toxicity, visual disturbance, peripheral edema; JNK inhibitors have limited clinical data; combination toxicity unknown

biomarker_match.rationale: Directly addresses SMAD4 loss molecular mechanism in this patient; SMAD4 loss drives MAPK/JNK activation causing treatment resistance; precision medicine approach

treatments.mechanism_of_action: SMAD4 loss in HNSCC activates compensatory MAPK/JNK prosurvival pathways, conferring resistance to EGFR inhibition. Dual blockade of EGFR (cetuximab/erlotinib) plus MEK or JNK inhibitors may overcome this resistance by targeting both the primary signaling axis and the bypass pathway.

key_evidence.study_name: SMAD4 Loss Is Associated with Cetuximab Resistance and Induction of MAPK/JNK Activation in HNSCC

treatments.name: Reduced-dose Radiation with Nivolumab (De-escalation — NOT Appropriate Here)

evidence_level.rationale: NRG HN002 Phase 2 data for low-risk HPV+ OPSCC; Phase 2/3 trial ongoing comparing reduced-dose RT + nivolumab vs standard cisplatin-CRT; applicable to favorable-risk patients ONLY

survival_benefit.rationale: 2-year PFS 90% with 60Gy + cisplatin in low-risk HPV+; this T4aN3b patient is HIGH-RISK and de-escalation is NOT appropriate; included for completeness but contraindicated

accessibility.rationale: Clinical trial access; restricted to favorable-risk HPV+ patients; this patient's T4aN3b disease with SMAD4 loss EXCLUDES eligibility

safety_profile.rationale: Reduced radiation dose (60 Gy vs 70 Gy) significantly decreases late toxicity including dysphagia and xerostomia; immune checkpoint replaces cisplatin toxicity

biomarker_match.rationale: CONTRAINDICATED for T4aN3b unfavorable-risk disease; designed for T1-T2 N0-N1 favorable HPV+ patients only; SMAD4 loss and PIK3CA mutation make de-escalation especially inappropriate

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Reduced radiation dose (60 Gy vs standard 70 Gy) with nivolumab replacing cisplatin as radiosensitizer aims to maintain cure rates while reducing long-term toxicity. NOT APPROPRIATE for T4aN3b unfavorable-risk disease — included for completeness only.
treatments.availability: Clinical Trial Only (NOT eligible for this patient)

treatments.name: Atezolizumab + Standard CRT (Clinical Trial)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2/3 clinical trial ongoing; adding anti-PD-L1 to standard CRT in locally advanced HNSCC; no published efficacy results yet

survival_benefit.rationale: Theoretical benefit of adding immune checkpoint blockade to standard CRT; may enhance radiation-induced immunogenic cell death; results pending

accessibility.rationale: Clinical trial access at participating centers; atezolizumab FDA-approved for other indications

safety_profile.rationale: Combined toxicity of CRT plus immune checkpoint inhibitor; immune-related AEs on top of radiation toxicity; manageable but additive

biomarker_match.rationale: Anti-PD-L1 approach; HPV+ tumors may have favorable tumor microenvironment for immunotherapy; SMAD4 loss impact on immunotherapy response unknown

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Atezolizumab blocks PD-L1, preventing its interaction with PD-1 on T cells. Adding this to standard cisplatin-CRT aims to leverage radiation-induced immunogenic cell death to generate anti-tumor immune responses while maintaining locoregional control through concurrent CRT.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase 2/3 Study of CRT + Atezolizumab in LA-HNSCC

treatments.name: Balstilimab (AGEN2034) Anti-PD-1 for HPV Clearance Monitoring

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 study at MD Anderson; novel anti-PD-1 agent evaluating HPV cfDNA clearance as response biomarker; early-stage investigation

survival_benefit.rationale: HPV cfDNA clearance as surrogate endpoint; potential to identify patients who achieve molecular complete response; may guide post-treatment surveillance

accessibility.rationale: Clinical trial access only at MD Anderson Cancer Center; investigational agent

safety_profile.rationale: Anti-PD-1 with expected class-effect immune-related AEs; generally well-tolerated based on class profile

biomarker_match.rationale: HPV plasma cfDNA monitoring directly relevant to HPV+ disease; innovative biomarker approach for treatment response assessment

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Balstilimab is a novel anti-PD-1 antibody being evaluated for its ability to clear HPV DNA from plasma (cell-free DNA) as a biomarker of treatment response. HPV cfDNA monitoring may provide a liquid biopsy approach to assess minimal residual disease after definitive therapy.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase 2 Study of Balstilimab for HPV cfDNA Clearance

clinical_trials.title: Neoadjuvant and Adjuvant Pembrolizumab in Resectable Locally Advanced HNSCC
clinical_trials.early_results: 36-month EFS 57.6% vs 46.4%; HR 0.66; FDA approved for PD-L1+ resectable LA-HNSCC

clinical_trials.title: Pembrolizumab +/- Chemotherapy for Recurrent/Metastatic HNSCC
clinical_trials.early_results: Median OS 13.0 vs 10.7 months with chemotherapy combo; ORR 36%

clinical_trials.title: Alpelisib in PIK3CA-Altered Recurrent/Metastatic HNSCC
biomarker_requirements: PIK3CA mutation or PI3K pathway alteration
clinical_trials.early_results: PFS 7.3 months for helical domain mutations; differential efficacy by mutation site

clinical_trials.title: PDS0101 (HPV Therapeutic Vaccine) Alone or with Pembrolizumab Before Surgery/RT for HPV+ OPSCC
clinical_trials.early_results: Window of opportunity trial; immune response data expected 2025-2026

clinical_trials.title: Reduced-Dose RT with Nivolumab vs Standard Cisplatin-CRT for Favorable-Risk HPV+ OPSCC
biomarker_requirements: HPV+ OPSCC, favorable risk ONLY (this patient NOT eligible)
clinical_trials.early_results: 2-year PFS 90% with 60Gy + cisplatin in favorable risk; NOT for T4aN3b disease

clinical_trials.title: ISA101b + Cemiplimab in HPV16+ Oropharyngeal Cancer
clinical_trials.early_results: HPV-specific T-cell responses observed; clinical response data pending

combination_strategies.evidence_level: Phase 3 RCT (RTOG 1016, De-ESCALaTE) — Standard of Care
combination_strategies.rationale: Gold standard for T4aN3b HPV+ OPSCC; cisplatin radiosensitization provides definitive locoregional control; de-escalation NOT appropriate for this high-volume unfavorable-risk disease
Perioperative immunotherapy with standard surgery/CRT improves EFS by 34% (HR 0.66); newly approved approach for PD-L1+ LA-HNSCC; applicable if surgical resection planned
For high-volume T4aN3b disease, induction TPF may debulk tumor before definitive CRT, potentially improving locoregional control and reducing distant failure risk
PIK3CA mutation present in this patient; PI3K pathway inhibition may radiosensitize or synergize with immunotherapy; TRIUMPH data support alpelisib activity in PIK3CA-mutant HNSCC

HPV-specific vaccine primes anti-viral T-cell responses; checkpoint inhibition removes immune brakes; radiation induces immunogenic cell death; triple combination targets HPV-driven oncogenesis from multiple angles

supportive_care.evidence: NCCN guidelines recommend considering prophylactic PEG for T4 oropharyngeal cancer undergoing CRT; reduces malnutrition risk and treatment interruptions
supportive_care.benefit: Maintains nutritional status during severe mucositis; prevents treatment breaks due to inability to swallow; improves treatment completion rates

supportive_care.evidence: ASCO/NCCN antiemesis guidelines; 5-HT3 antagonist + NK1 antagonist + dexamethasone for highly emetogenic cisplatin regimen
supportive_care.benefit: Prevents severe nausea/vomiting from cisplatin 100mg/m2; maintains oral intake; reduces dehydration and treatment delays

supportive_care.evidence: Multiple RCTs showing prophylactic swallowing exercises during CRT preserve long-term swallowing function; ASHA clinical practice guidelines
supportive_care.benefit: Reduces long-term dysphagia severity; maintains swallowing muscle function during radiation fibrosis; prevents aspiration pneumonia; improves quality of life

supportive_care.evidence: Standard pre-radiation dental assessment prevents osteoradionecrosis; fluoride trays reduce radiation caries
supportive_care.benefit: Prevents osteoradionecrosis of the mandible; reduces dental caries; comprehensive dental clearance before initiating radiation to irradiated fields

supportive_care.evidence: Multiple studies showing HPV cfDNA as a sensitive biomarker for treatment response and surveillance in HPV+ OPSCC
supportive_care.benefit: Liquid biopsy for minimal residual disease detection; potential to replace or augment imaging surveillance; early detection of recurrence

supportive_care.evidence: NCCN distress management guidelines; evidence for psychological support reducing treatment-related anxiety and depression
supportive_care.benefit: Although this patient is a never-smoker, psychosocial support addresses treatment anxiety, body image concerns, and sexual health issues related to HPV diagnosis
